title: McCarthy offers deal to end standoff in House speaker fight
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The contours of a deal that could make Republican leader Kevin McCarthy the House speaker have begun to emerge after three grueling days and 11 failed votes in a political spectacle unseen in a century.

title: 2 years after Jan. 6, speaker scrap paralyzes Congress again
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Such are the fractures in the country, between the political parties and inside the Republican Party itself, that one time-honored specialty of Washington — memorializing and coming together over national trauma — isn’t what it used to be.

title: As speaker bid falters, mixed views of McCarthy in hometown
shortDescription: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (AP) — House Republican leader Kevin McCarthy’s political troubles don’t stop at the Washington Beltway. In his conservative, Central California hometown of Bakersfield – where oil derricks blanket hillsides and country music fans flock to Buck Owens’ Crystal Palace hall – some voters are asking if what has become an embarrassing bid to succeed Democratic House Speaker Nancy Pelosi came at the expense of the twin engines of the local economy – oil production and agriculture.

title: California’s Newsom to launch 2nd term with contrast to GOP
shortDescription: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom will kick off his second and final term on Friday by contrasting his leadership of the nation's largest Democratic stronghold with Republican leaders he's branded as threats to freedom and democracy — including former President Donald Trump.

title: Biden toughens border, offers legal path for 30,000 a month
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Thursday the U.S. would immediately begin turning away Cubans, Haitians and Nicaraguans who cross the border from Mexico illegally, his boldest move yet to confront the arrivals of migrants that have spiraled since he took office two years ago.

title: Stabenow’s retirement scrambles Michigan Senate race in 2024
shortDescription: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Sen. Debbie Stabenow, a member of the Democratic leadership, announced Thursday that she will not run for a fifth term in 2024, a surprise decision that sets up a scramble by both parties for an open seat in the key battleground state.

title: EXPLAINER: Capitol riot investigation growing 2 years later
shortDescription: The largest investigation in the Justice Department's history keeps growing two years after a violent mob of supporters of then-President Donald Trump attacked the U.S.

title: Biden to award Citizens Medal to 12 on Jan. 6 anniversary
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Friday will present the nation's second highest civilian award to 12 individuals involved in defending the Capitol during the insurrection on Jan.

title: Biden toughens border, offers legal path for 30,000 a month
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Thursday the U.S. would immediately begin turning away Cubans, Haitians and Nicaraguans who cross the border from Mexico illegally, his boldest move yet to confront the arrivals of migrants that have spiraled since he took office two years ago.

title: Japan, US to hold security talks before Kishida meets Biden
shortDescription: TOKYO (AP) — Japan and the United States will hold security talks between their foreign and defense ministers in Washington the day before Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida lands in the U.S. capital next week wrapping up his tour of Group of Seven nations as Tokyo expands its military and deepens its alliance with America amid China's growing influence.

title: Idaho court tosses lawsuits aiming to block abortion bans
shortDescription: Idaho's Constitution does not implicitly enshrine abortion as a fundamental right, the state Supreme Court ruled Thursday as it dismissed a series of lawsuits brought by Planned Parenthood.

title: Mexico nabs son of drug lord ‘El Chapo’ before Biden visit
shortDescription: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican security forces captured Ovidio Guzmán, an alleged drug trafficker wanted by the United States and one of the sons of former Sinaloa cartel boss Joaquín “El Chapo” Guzmán, in a pre-dawn operation Thursday that set off gunfights and roadblocks across the western state’s capital.

title: McCarthy fails for 3rd day in bitter GOP House speaker fight
Force behind Arizona’s ‘show me your papers’ law dead at 75
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Russell Pearce, a Republican lawmaker who was the driving force behind Arizona's landmark 2010 immigration legislation known as the “show me your papers” law and other anti-immigrant measures, has died.

title: ‘Bomb cyclone’ brings damaging winds, drenches California
shortDescription: CAPITOLA, Calif. (AP) — Hurricane-force winds, surging surf and heavy rains from a powerful “atmospheric river” pounded California on Thursday, knocking out power to tens of thousands, causing flooding, and contributing to the deaths of at least two people, including a toddler whose home was crushed by a falling tree.

title: Judge: Tennessee must release consultant COVID response docs
shortDescription: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee judge has ordered Gov. Bill Lee’s administration to release consultant reports that recommend how to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic — documents the state argued should remain secret under public records law.

title: South Carolina Supreme Court strikes down state abortion ban
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — The South Carolina Supreme Court on Thursday struck down a ban on abortion after six weeks, ruling the restriction enacted by the Deep South state violates a state constitutional right to privacy.

title: Biden agenda, lithium mine, tribes, greens collide in Nevada
shortDescription: RENO, Nev. (AP) — Opponents of the largest lithium mine planned in the U.S. urged a federal judge in Nevada on Thursday to vacate the U.S. government's approval of the project until it completes additional environmental reviews and complies with all state and federal laws.

title: Biden visits Vatican embassy to pay respects to Benedict XVI
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Thursday paid his respects to Benedict XVI, the first pope to resign in six centuries, following his burial during a ceremony at the Vatican.Benedict, who became pope emeritus after stepping down from his role as leader of the Roman Catholic church in 2013, died Dec.

title: GOP-led Kentucky House passes top-priority tax cut measure
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The Kentucky House voted Thursday to build on efforts to cut the state's individual income tax rate, as Republicans took another step toward achieving a long-running policy objective.

title: Biden signs bill to ease costs for prisoner calls to family
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Thursday signed into law a bill aimed at easing the cost for prisoners to call family and friends.The legislation clarifies that the Federal Communications Commission, which regulates interstate and international communications through cable, radio, television, satellite and wire, can set limits for fees on audio and video calls inside corrections facilities.

title: Vermont GOP governor sworn in to 4th term in blue state
shortDescription: MONTPELIER, Vt. (AP) — Vermont Republican Gov. Phil Scott began his fourth two-year term Thursday by urging the state’s lawmakers to help find ways to even the playing field between the state’s most well-off communities and those that are being left behind.

title: Abortion rights bill fast-tracked in Minnesota to become law
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — A bill to strengthen abortion rights in Minnesota is on the fast track to becoming law as it passed its first test Thursday.A House health panel approved the legislation, which codifies protections into state statutes, 11-8 on just the third day of the 2023 session and sent it to its next committee stop on an expedited path to a House floor vote.

title: Craft deflects questions about Trump as she files for Ky gov
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Former United Nations Ambassador Kelly Craft shied away from talking about her ex-boss — Donald Trump — and his support for a political rival Thursday as she formally entered the crowded Republican gubernatorial primary in Kentucky.

title: Recap: House speaker vote fails again, impasse breaks 100-year record
shortDescription: The vote for a new speaker of the House dragged into day three Thursday as Republican leader Rep. Kevin McCarthy lost a series of ballots.

title: Biden says Mexico will step up help on border
shortDescription: Ahead of his anticipated border trip, Biden said Mexico will accept the return of undocumented migrants from Venezuela, Cuba, Haiti and Nicaragua.

title: Arrest of El Chapo's son results in Mexican cartel violence before Biden visit
shortDescription: Days before Biden's trip to Mexico, the country spiraled into violence after the arrest of Ovidio Guzmán, son of infamous drug lord known as "El Chapo."

title: OnPolitics: As House speaker vote drags on, what can be done?
shortDescription: After nine rounds of voting, Rep. Kevin McCarthy still doesn't have the votes for House speaker. What happens now?

title: Here are the 12 people being honored by Biden on the Jan. 6 anniversary
shortDescription: Biden is giving one of the nation's highest civilian honors to 12 people who showed "courage and selflessness" related to the Jan. 6 attacks.

title: Meet Byron Donalds, the GOP representative nominated for House speaker
shortDescription: Republican opponents to GOP Rep. Kevin McCarthy as House speaker nominated Florida Rep. Byron Donalds for speaker of the House as voting continues.

title: Biden to visit the border for the first time
shortDescription: President Joe Biden unveiled new steps aimed at stemming historic migration as he plans to visit El Paso, Texas.

title: What happens after McCarthy loses speaker votes? The process explained
shortDescription: Candidates for speaker are nominated from the floor by members of their parties. The speaker who is chosen does not have to be a member of Congress.

title: House speaker election creates viral moments
shortDescription: Members of Congress voiced frustration as GOP members continue to battle over who should be the next speaker of the House.

title: 'We all look like domestic terrorists': How Jan. 6 shook loyalties of Trump aides
shortDescription: Some of Donald Trump's aides sought desperately to get him to denounce violence, others were frustrated by his failure to abide by election results.

title: Jan. 6 attack 2 years later: Trump still plagued by multiple investigations
shortDescription: Two years after the deadly Capitol attack, Trump’s potential legal exposure has only grown more perilous, with his future political viability unknown.

title: Biden and McConnell appear together, striking contrast to Kevin McCarthy House chaos
shortDescription: McConnell hailed a $1.6 billion bridge project for Kentucky and Ohio a legislative miracle.' Biden said it proves "we can work together."

title: Congress' 2nd day takeaways: McCarthy keeps losing, Biden mocks GOP
shortDescription: The ongoing defeats came despite pleas to the rebel faction from former President Donald Trump and former Vice President Mike Pence to back McCarthy.

title: McCarthy at a loss in speaker vote
shortDescription: The failure to elect a House speaker on the initial ballot hasn't happened in a century.

title: Biden urges Supreme Court to uphold student loan forgiveness program
shortDescription:
Biden urged the Supreme Court to uphold his stalled student loan forgiveness plan in a brief filed in 
pair of suits challenging its legality.

title: OnPolitics: House still in chaos as Biden says 'it's not my problem'
shortDescription: Biden preaches bipartisanship with McConnell, tweeting "when we work together, we can get things done.” It's a sharp contrast to the House floor.

title: Biden to visit border for first time in his presidency en route to Mexico
shortDescription: President Biden says it's his 'intention' to visit the U.S.-Mexico border.

title: Biden praises McConnell for bipartisan work
shortDescription: President Joe Biden praised Minority Leader Mitch McConnell for his bipartisan cooperation to pass infrastructure legislation in Covington, Kentucky.

title: Why DeSantis and Republicans have declared war on corporate America
shortDescription: Republicans like Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis are waging war against "corporate wokeness" from CRT to ESG to score political points with their base.

title: FBI ups reward in Capitol pipe bomb probe to $500K
shortDescription: Pipe bombs were planted near the Republican and Democratic national committee headquarters the night before the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack.

title: New Hampshire Democrats to DNC: Please don't punish us in 2024 primary
shortDescription: New Hampshire Democrats say they have no ability to change state election laws to comply with the DNC's 2024 presidential nominating calendar mandate.

title: Meet Hakeem Jeffries, Pelosi's successor for Democratic leadership
shortDescription: While speaker vote results remain unclear on the Republican side, Democrats have chosen their man: Representative Hakeem Jeffries.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: Republicans’ Internecine Conflict Is Mirrored in Conservative Media
shortDescription: National Review has called the House leadership votes “an embarrassing spectacle.” Others have cheered it on.By Jeremy W. Peters

title: Possible Senate rivals split over McCarthy
shortDescription: GOP House members in Indiana and Montana are going opposite ways on the speaker race ahead of potential Senate primaries.
